zh.lifeSummary: Joan de Plecy是John dePlecy和Elizabeth de Havering的女兒，出生在英國多瑟特沙維其可。她於1409年與John Camell在英國多瑟特費德利福德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1465年在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan de Plecy是John dePlecy和Elizabeth de Havering的女儿，出生在英国多瑟特沙維其可。她于1409年与John Camell在英国多瑟特費德利福德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1465年在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Plecy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Norwegian Lyse: habitational name from the farm name Lyse, mainly in Rogaland, derived from Old Norse ljóss ‘light’, referring to a river with light or clear water.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Joan de Plecy was born in Shapwick, Dorset, England as the daughter of John dePlecy and Elizabeth de Havering. She married John Camell in 1409, in Fiddleford, Dorset, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1465, in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan de Plecy est née à Shapwick, Dorset, Angleterre, fille de John dePlecy et Elizabeth de Havering. Elle a épousé John Camell en 1409, à Fiddleford, Dorset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1465, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Joan de Plecy wurde in Shapwick, Dorset, England, als Tochter von John dePlecy und Elizabeth de Havering geboren. Sie hat 1409 in Fiddleford, Dorset, England John Camell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1465 in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Joan de Plecy è nata il nel Shapwick, Dorset, England come figlia di John dePlecy e Elizabeth de Havering. Lei sposato John Camell nel 1409, nel Fiddleford, Dorset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1465, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de PlecyはイギリスドールセトシャプウィックでJohn dePlecyとElizabeth de Haveringの娘として生まれた。1409年にイギリスドールセトフィッドレフォードでJohn Camellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1465年に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Plecy(은)는 영국 도르세트 샤p윅에서 John dePlecy(와)과 Elizabeth de Havering의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1409년 영국 도르세트 피들레퍼드에서 John Camell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1465년 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joan de Plecy nasceu em Shapwick, Dorset, Inglaterra filha de John dePlecy e Elizabeth de Havering. Ela casou-se com John Camell em 1409, em Fiddleford, Dorset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1465, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan de Plecy родилась в местности шапвикк, дорсет, Англия. Её родителей звали John dePlecy и Elizabeth de Havering. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Camell в 1409 г. в местности фиддлефорд, дорсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1465 г. в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Joan de Plecy nació en Shapwick, Dorset, Inglaterra como hija de John dePlecy y Elizabeth de Havering. Se casó con John Camell en 1409, en Fiddleford, Dorset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1465, en su pueblo.
